top_findings.title: Date range filtering not exposed via MCP; season filtering broken in MCP layer
top_findings.evidence: query.clj:55-60 defines matches-by-date-range but no MCP tool exposes start/end date parameters. Additionally, mcp.clj:83 uses cond->> to thread matches through (q/matches-by-season season), but cond->> appends the accumulated value as the last arg, calling (matches-by-season season-int matches-vec) instead of (matches-by-season matches-vec season-int). The competition and team filters on lines 85-86 correctly use anonymous fn wrappers (#(...)) but season does not. Season filtering works when called directly (query_test.clj:39) but fails via MCP dispatch.
top_findings.suggestion: Add date_start/date_end params to team_matches tool schema and wire to matches-by-date-range. Fix mcp.clj:83 to use an anonymous fn: (#(q/matches-by-season % season)) matching the pattern used for competition and team.

top_findings.title: filter-matches cond->> passes season with wrong argument order to matches-by-season
top_findings.evidence: mcp.clj:83 — `season (q/matches-by-season season)` inside cond->> threads the match collection as the last arg, producing (matches-by-season season-value matches-coll). But matches-by-season (query.clj:48-49) expects [matches season]. Affects team_matches, team_record, standings, biggest_wins, and statistics MCP tools when season param is provided. Tests pass because query_test.clj calls matches-by-season directly with correct arg order, never exercising the MCP dispatch path.
top_findings.suggestion: Change mcp.clj:83 from `season (q/matches-by-season season)` to `season (#(q/matches-by-season % season))` to match the pattern used for competition and team on lines 85-86.
